attacking_style: Classic bunch sprint finisher who relies on staying sheltered until the final meters, launching sprint in protected position rather than long-range efforts.
team_tactics: Depends on leadout train for positioning and shelter; avoids early exposure to wind, letting rivals commit first from distance (800m+).
terrain_preference: Flat bunch sprint finishes where leadout organization and tactical positioning determine outcome; struggles when forced into wind early.
vulnerability: Confidence-dependent performer whose head drops during result droughts; vulnerable when exposed to wind prematurely or lacking leadout support.
key_patterns: Wins from sheltered position while rivals forced early into wind (800m+ out).
Performance heavily tied to confidence; extended periods without results cause mental struggles.
Relies on tactical positioning over raw power; waits for others to commit first.
form_trajectory: positive: broke significant results drought with Tour de Pologne win, providing needed confidence boost after lean spell post-Giro
